title: WaiverPro - Healthcare Waiver Management
paragraphs: Streamline your regulatory waiver process from submission to approval

Apply when the facility is seeking a waiver of the 2.4 CNA requirement to meet individual patient needs but will provide a minimum of 3.5 direct care service hours. Facilities may only submit one Patient Needs Waiver and/or one Workforce Shortage Waiver application in the same year. If submitting both, only one may be approved.

Apply when the facility is seeking program flexibility to regulations. The request must include justification for the program flexibility, adequate description of the alternative concept, and documentation supporting that patient care will not be compromised. The facility may request program flexibility that is intended to be emergency or routine. An approved program flexibility request is for a limited term with a start and an end date. Facilities may submit more than one application as needed.
This waiver was available from Dec 29, 2025 to Apr 30, 2026

Apply when the facility is seeking a waiver of the 3.5 direct care service hours requirement and/or the 2.4 CNA hours requirement due to a workforce shortage of available and appropriate health care professionals and direct care givers. Facilities may only submit one Patient Needs Waiver and/or one Workforce Shortage Waiver application in the same year. If submitting both, only one may be approved.
list_items: Applies to individualized patient care plan modifications based on clinical needs
Documented patient or family request for accommodation required
Clinical assessment supporting the modification must be on file
Interdisciplinary team agreement and approval is required
Submit a detailed justification demonstrating how the alternative maintains compliance and protects residents
All requests must be submitted online through WaiverPRO
CDPH will respond within 60 days after all required documentation is received
Approved flexibilities include an expiration date and specific terms; may be revoked if conditions are not met
Facilities may not staff below 3.2 direct care hours per patient day, even with an approved waiver
Only licensed freestanding SNFs (excluding GACH distinct parts, state-owned hospitals, and developmental centers) may apply
Application Window: January 1 – April 1 annually; applications will not be accepted outside this timeframe
One Workforce Shortage Waiver and/or one Patient Needs Waiver per year; only one may be approved

Patient Needs Waiver (PNW) Request

Apply when the facility is seeking a waiver of the 2.4 CNA requirement to meet individual patient needs but will provide a minimum of 3.5 direct care service hours. Facilities may only submit one Patient Needs Waiver and/or one Workforce Shortage Waiver application in the same year. If submitting both, only one may be approved.

15–20 business days
Processing Time
6–12 months
Waiver Period
Applies to individualized patient care plan modifications based on clinical needs
Documented patient or family request for accommodation required
Clinical assessment supporting the modification must be on file
Interdisciplinary team agreement and approval is required
Program Flexibility (PF) Request

Apply when the facility is seeking program flexibility to regulations. The request must include justification for the program flexibility, adequate description of the alternative concept, and documentation supporting that patient care will not be compromised. The facility may request program flexibility that is intended to be emergency or routine. An approved program flexibility request is for a limited term with a start and an end date. Facilities may submit more than one application as needed.

Within 60 days
Processing Time
6–12 months
Waiver Period
Submit a detailed justification demonstrating how the alternative maintains compliance and protects residents
All requests must be submitted online through WaiverPRO
CDPH will respond within 60 days after all required documentation is received
Approved flexibilities include an expiration date and specific terms; may be revoked if conditions are not met
Workforce Shortage Waiver (WSW) Request
Expired

This waiver was available from Dec 29, 2025 to Apr 30, 2026

Apply when the facility is seeking a waiver of the 3.5 direct care service hours requirement and/or the 2.4 CNA hours requirement due to a workforce shortage of available and appropriate health care professionals and direct care givers. Facilities may only submit one Patient Needs Waiver and/or one Workforce Shortage Waiver application in the same year. If submitting both, only one may be approved.

15–20 business days
Processing Time
6–12 months
Waiver Period
Facilities may not staff below 3.2 direct care hours per patient day, even with an approved waiver
Only licensed freestanding SNFs (excluding GACH distinct parts, state-owned hospitals, and developmental centers) may apply
Application Window: January 1 – April 1 annually; applications will not be accepted outside this timeframe
One Workforce Shortage Waiver and/or one Patient Needs Waiver per year; only one may be approved
